title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) are an inference-time compute paradigm that can be applied to coding agents for higher performance and reliability. The presentation discusses mapping RLM principles to coding agents and demonstrates how Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse guide recursive task-solving with declarable outcomes.
writeup: This talk from AI Engineer maps Recursive Language Models (RLMs), an inference-time compute paradigm, onto coding agents to improve performance and reliability. Raymond Weitekamp of OpenProse explains how RLM principles translate to agentic coding work, then demonstrates Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse guiding recursive task-solving with declarable outcomes. The takeaway: recursion is framed as a test-time compute lever for coding agents, with declarable outcomes structuring how tasks decompose and resolve.
